summary: Iteration 3 — TokenForge enhancement testing (quota meter, ROI PDF, annual billing, usage alert banner). 100% green: 33/33 backend pytest cases (27 regression + 6 new enhancement tests) and 6/6 UI flows via Playwright on the preview URL.

design_issues.screen: Billing /dashboard/billing — Enterprise card on annual cycle
issues: Long subtext '/mo, billed annually' wraps to a new line and visually splits ($399.20 ... annually) — not a bug but slightly awkward. Consider shortening to '/mo annual' or letting the card grow wider on lg screens.

Non-blocking React warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' originates in Dashboard render. Likely a .map() in the recent-logs or KPI grid missing key={...}. Cosmetic but should be cleaned up.

action_items: (Optional/minor) Add missing key prop in Dashboard list render to silence React console warning.
(Optional/cosmetic) Tighten Enterprise card subtext so '/mo, billed annually' does not wrap awkwardly on 3-col grid.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /reports/savings.pdf imports reportlab inside the endpoint — fine for cold-path PDF, but consider top-level import to avoid per-request import overhead and surface ImportError at boot rather than at first request.

server.py /proxy/chat quota check runs an aggregation on every request via get_user_usage(). For high-RPS this is O(N) per call. Consider caching usage in user doc (atomic $inc on tokens_used) for cheaper enforcement at scale.
/billing/plans now correctly returns 'free' tier from backend (single source of truth) — Pricing.jsx no longer needs to synthesize it. Confirmed FE consumes backend list directly.
Pricing.jsx displayPrice strips '.00' on monthly amount but not on annual (e.g. shows '79.20' but also strips would yield '79.20'). Consistent enough; no action needed.
Dashboard.jsx defensive default for user?.usage is correct (avoids NPE when /auth/me hasn't returned yet).
test_credentials: Admin admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026. Dynamically created QA users TEST_qa_quota_<hex>@tokenforge.io / qaforge123 (backend) and qa_ui_<ts>_<rnd>@tokenforge.io / qaforge123 (UI).
seed_data_creation: Backend pytest: 1 fresh QA user (auto API key), 1 Stripe annual checkout session (pro plan, ~$950.40) persisted to payment_transactions with billing_cycle='annual'. UI test: 1 fresh QA UI user, 1 proxy_requests row inserted via mongosh to drive quota meter to 84% then quota mutated to 10 to hit 100% banner (restored to 50,000 after test). No production / admin data modified. All test rows are prefixed TEST_ or carry the qa_ui_* email.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 enhancement features verified end-to-end on https://market-launch-43.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend tests are split across two files: test_tokenforge_backend.py (regression) and test_tokenforge_enhancements.py (iter-3). Run together with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Frontend register page uses data-testid='register-email/password/name/submit' (NOT reg-*). Quota banner can be simulated by inserting a proxy_requests row + lowering users.monthly_quota in MongoDB then refreshing /dashboard. PDF download via /api/reports/savings.pdf returns valid PDF (~2.8 KB for new user with no usage). Stripe checkout in test mode for annual Pro returns amount 950.40 and billing_cycle=annual.
rca_of_the_issue:
N/A — no failures. Detailed verifications:
- GET /auth/me returns usage{period_start, tokens_used, requests, tokens_saved, monthly_quota, percent_used} with correct types — verified.
- POST /proxy/chat returns HTTP 429 with detail starting 'Monthly quota exceeded' once usage.tokens_used >= user.monthly_quota — reproduced by shrinking quota to 1 in MongoDB; quota restored after test.
- GET /billing/plans returns 4 plans (free, starter, pro, enterprise) with annual_amount = round(amount*12*0.8, 2) and annual_discount_pct=20.
- POST /billing/checkout with billing_cycle='annual' for pro returned amount=950.40 and persisted billing_cycle='annual' in payment_transactions (verified via direct Mongo read).
- GET /reports/savings.pdf returns 401 without auth, 200 with admin Bearer; Content-Type application/pdf, Content-Disposition contains 'attachment', body starts with b'%PDF', size > 1KB (2798 bytes for fresh user).
- Dashboard: quota-meter, quota-used, quota-total rendered correctly; download-report-btn triggers /api/reports/savings.pdf and receives valid PDF.
- Dashboard banner: hidden at 0%, WARNING styling at 84% (text 'QUOTA WARNING'), EXCEEDED styling at 100% (text 'QUOTA EXCEEDED').
- Pricing: billing-cycle-toggle switches Pro card from '$99/mo' to '$79.20/mo, billed annually' with '$950.40 billed yearly' subtext.
- Billing: bill-cycle-annual toggle works; clicking billing-buy-pro sends {plan_id:'pro', billing_cycle:'annual'} POST body to /api/billing/checkout.
